title: Tree, Node, TreeCursor, GSS stack, and subtree node creation
description:
Implement the core data structures needed by the parser. These can be unit tested independently before the parser loop is built.

Deliverables:
- stack.go: Graph-Structured Stack (GSS) with StackNode (state, links[8], position, errorCost, nodeCount, dynamicPrecedence), StackLink (node, subtree, isPending). Operations: push, pop (fan out across merged links, bounded by MAX_ITERATOR_COUNT=64), split (fork version), merge (combine equivalent versions), pause/resume (error recovery), halt (discard version). Use sync.Pool or manual free-list for StackNode. Replace C callback pattern in stack_iter with direct loop + enum return.
- tree.go: Tree struct (root Subtree, language *Language, includedRanges []Range, arenas []*SubtreeArena). Node value type (~24 bytes: context [4]uint32, id SubtreeID, tree *Tree). Node accessors: Type, Symbol, StartByte, EndByte, StartPoint, EndPoint, ChildCount, NamedChildCount, Child, NamedChild, ChildByFieldName, Parent, NextSibling, PrevSibling, String (S-expression).
- tree_cursor.go: TreeCursor with stack-based traversal. TreeCursorEntry (subtree, position, childIndex, structuralChildIndex, descendantIndex). GotoFirstChild, GotoNextSibling, GotoParent, CurrentNode. Pre-allocate cursor stack with capacity 32.
- subtree.go additions: internal node creation (ts_subtree_new_node), ts_subtree_summarize_children (compute visibleChildCount, namedChildCount, visibleDescendantCount, firstLeaf, repeatDepth, etc).

Tests:
- Stack operations: push, pop with multiple paths, version merging, version halting
- TreeCursor traversal correctness (manually constructed trees)
- Node accessor correctness (positions, child navigation, field names)
- S-expression output
- Internal node creation and summarize_children correctness

Reference: docs/design.md §2, C source: stack.c, node.c, tree_cursor.c, tree.c, subtree.c
